steps.title: Determine what the blank must mean
steps.content:
The sentence argues that “there is little that can ______ being the first to do something” — meaning almost nothing can outdo, beat, or surpass being first. The Wanderwell sentence reinforces this: people remember firsts. The blank needs a phrase meaning “surpass” or “triumph over.”
  • Choice A — 'overreach by': “Overreach by being first” means to go too far by being first — the opposite of the intended meaning. ✗
  • Choice B — 'fluctuate with': To vary alongside something; carries no sense of surpassing. ✗
  • Choice C — 'constrain within': To limit or confine; unrelated to the idea of outcompeting. ✗
  • Choice D — 'prevail over': To triumph over or prove superior to — exactly what the sentence needs: little can triumph over being first. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the word choice
steps.content: “Prevail over” captures the meaning that almost nothing beats being the first to accomplish something, which aligns with the Wanderwell example. That matches choice D — D is correct.
